- Implication: costed intraspecific aggression EMERGES on the discrete patch substrate — it is genuinely selected (invades + positive local gradient), scarcity-gated, with no reward and a real cost. This CLEARS the local-gradient wall that closed the population-ecology sense-evolution arc (where seven levers all failed to make a costed sense invade). The wall is therefore TRAIT-SPECIFIC: a costed behavior whose benefit is the direct seizure of a competitor's scarce reproduction clears it, where a costed perceptual organ did not. Answers the human's R1 question — "can creatures figure out to fight each other without hard-coding it?" — YES, under scarcity. Opens the ladder (R2 scarcity-map + predator integration -> R3 cannibalism/roles -> R4 grouping/cooperation).
- Honest caveat: POSITIVE-SINGLE / NEW INSIGHT, scoped. (1) Trait EXPRESSIBILITY (contest = f(aggr)) is by-construction; the EMERGENT result is that the costed behavior is SELECTED + scarcity-gated + clears the wall. (2) The 50/50 common-garden at resident aggr=0 is NOT scarcity-gated (always-win-from-50% artifact); scarcity-gating is shown by invasion-from-rarity (9/10 vs 0/10) and by the higher-resident gradient (aggr=0.3: POS scarce, NEG abundant) — reported honestly, not hidden. (3) Predator frozen + weak/strong as the scarcity knob; prey-vs-prey only (R1 scope); cooperation/roles are later rungs. (4) Reproduction-opportunity-under-crowding is the currency (no explicit energy buffer — it destabilized the continuous substrate). Self-grade: POSITIVE-SINGLE.
- Verdict: POSITIVE-SINGLE — costed prey-vs-prey contest aggression EMERGES under reproduction scarcity (invades 9/10 scarce vs 0/10 abundant; positive local gradient 8/8 scarce; scarcity-gated POS-scarce/NEG-abundant at resident aggr=0.3), clearing the local-gradient WALL that closed population-ecology. Not hard-coded (no reward, real cost via cost-null, drift-null neutral 4/8). First costed-BEHAVIOR emergence in the program. R1 done; the wall is trait-specific.
- Verifier: controller designed the substrate (built via subagent-driven TDD, controller re-verified byte-identity goldens d063c91+790a8499 after each task + the contest live/inert/cost properties), designed + ran the experiment, and adjudicated the raw PER-SEED rows itself (mean-of-opposites-guard): caught the abundance-gradient anomaly at resident aggr=0, traced it to the always-win-from-50% artifact, and confirmed clean scarcity-gating via a higher-resident check (aggr=0.3 POS-scarce/NEG-abundant) + invasion-from-rarity (9/10 vs 0/10).
- Next: R2 — map the scarcity-dependence (basin of P(aggressive lineage) vs predation pressure) at a realistic resident aggr, then integrate the contest into the full co-evolving predator-prey system (does intraspecific competition buffer the Exp-259 prey collapse?). Then R3 cannibalism/roles, R4 grouping/cooperation.
- trace: experiments/exp263_intraspecific_contest_emergence.py -> experiments/outputs/exp263.txt (gated enable_contest + heritable aggr + lineage tracking in ecology/patchmosaic.py, byte-identical OFF; reuses ecology/evolvability/metrics.py; applies skill mean-of-opposites-guard; substrate built via subagent-driven-development)

## Exp 264 — R2: the emerged contest aggression IN CONTEXT — scarcity basin map (clean) + predator integration: intraspecific aggression robustly CO-EMERGES with the co-evolving predator (NOT washed out — refuges supply local crowding) but is ORTHOGONAL to it (does NOT buffer the Exp-259 prey-escape collapse) (MIXED / NEW INSIGHT). Direction: emergent-intraspecific-competition.
- Plain: R1 (Exp 263) showed costed prey-vs-prey contest aggression emerges under scarcity. R2 puts it IN CONTEXT. Part A maps the scarcity basin: P(aggression invades from rarity) vs frozen predation pressure (attack_a sweep). Part B is the novel question: turn the predator's attack trait back ON and let prey escape AND aggression co-evolve WITH it (3-trait co-evolution) on the Exp-259 collapse-prone BOTH regime; single causal bit = enable_contest (OFF = Exp-259-like escape+attack, aggr inert; ON = prey also contest). Does intraspecific contest change the predator-prey outcome (buffer the prey-escape collapse), or get washed out (a strong predator suppresses prey below K -> abundance -> no crowding -> no contest)?
- Consolidation vs new insight: MIXED / NEW INSIGHT. Consolidates R1 (Part A basin map confirms scarcity-gating + locates the transition). NEW insight: intraspecific aggression robustly CO-EMERGES with the predator-prey arms race (the local refuges supply the crowding that global predation removes — so it is NOT washed out), but it is ORTHOGONAL — it does not buffer/resolve the Exp-259 predator-dominance; the predator-prey co-evolution is essentially unchanged by adding the third trait.
- Question: (A) how does P(aggression invades) vary with predation pressure? (B) does intraspecific contest (single bit = enable_contest) change the co-evolving predator-prey outcome? FALSIFIER for "contest matters in context": if ON and OFF are indistinguishable on esc/atk/pops/extinction AND aggr@end stays ~0 under the co-evolving predator, the emerged aggression is WASHED OUT.
- Setup: experiments/exp264_contest_in_context.py (multiprocessing). Part A: invasion-from-rarity (rare aggr=0.5 into aggr=0 resident, predator FROZEN; reuses exp263.invasion — NOT the resident-0 gradient, to dodge the always-win artifact) across attack_a {0.01,0.02,0.04,0.06,0.08}, seeds 620-629. Part B: 3-trait co-evolution (escape+aggr+attack all evolving, freeze none, mutation_rate=0.15) on the Exp-259 BOTH regime (refuge access=0.30/frac=0.25, migration=0.05, async rotating amp=0.4, attack_a=0.05/K_pred=40/hmax=0.05, escape_cost=0.15, contest_cost=0.10, contest_seize=0.50), horizon=3000, seeds 720-729, contest OFF vs ON. Per-seed dispersion reported (mean-of-opposites-guard).
- Result: MIXED. (A) BASIN MAP: P(invade) = 9/10, 9/10, 8/10, 0/10, 2/10 across attack_a 0.01->0.08 — clean scarcity-gating, transition at attack_a ~0.04-0.06 (weak predation -> prey crowd -> aggression invades; strong predation -> prey suppressed -> it does not). (B) PREDATOR INTEGRATION: aggression ROBUSTLY co-emerges under the co-evolving predator — aggr@end = 0.857 mean, 0.70-0.91 in ALL 10 ON seeds (NOT washed out; the refuges supply local crowding). BUT contest does NOT change the predator-prey outcome: ON vs OFF escape is statistically indistinguishable — both BIMODAL (per-seed esc OFF mostly ~0.5 with 3/10 escaped ~3.5; ON mostly ~0.5 with 2/10 escaped ~3.5; means 1.498 vs 1.168 within sd~1.3-1.4, a mean-of-opposites both arms), attack ~2.0 both (2.010 vs 2.087), 0/10 extinct both, ON slightly lower prey (336 vs 381, the contest cost). The 2-seed smoke's apparent "buffering" (esc 2.37 vs 0.52) was a mean-of-opposites artifact, caught by the full 10-seed per-seed data.
- Implication: intraspecific aggression and predator-prey co-evolution are LARGELY ORTHOGONAL on this substrate. Aggression robustly emerges even with a strong co-evolving predator (so R1's emergence is not a no-predator artifact — the local refuges decouple intraspecific crowding from global predation pressure), but it neither buffers nor destabilises the predator-prey arms race: coexistence holds (0/10 extinct both arms) and the bistable predator-dominance-vs-prey-escape outcome (consistent with Exp-261/262 bistability) is unchanged by adding the third trait. So "introduce intraspecific competition into the predator-prey system" gives a THIRD co-emerging trait, not a regime change. The buffering hypothesis is NOT supported; the washed-out hypothesis is FALSIFIED.
- Honest caveat: MIXED / NEW INSIGHT, scoped. (1) Both Part-B arms are bimodal (predator-dom vs prey-escaped) — the ON-vs-OFF comparison is read on per-seed distributions + escaped-fraction, NOT the pooled mean (mean-of-opposites-guard); the means (1.50 vs 1.17) are not reliable and the difference is within seed noise. (2) The smoke (2 seeds) misleadingly showed buffering — a concrete instance of the mean-of-opposites trap, resolved by the full per-seed data. (3) trait EXPRESSIBILITY by-construction; the emergent results are the basin map + aggr's robust co-emergence + the orthogonality. (4) "Orthogonal" is for THIS regime (refuge-bearing BOTH); a refuge-free regime might couple them (predation would then control crowding directly) — a follow-up.
- Verdict: MIXED / NEW INSIGHT — (A) scarcity basin map clean (P(invade) 9/10 scarce -> 0/10 abundant, transition attack_a~0.04-0.06). (B) intraspecific aggression robustly CO-EMERGES with the co-evolving predator (aggr@end 0.857, 0.70-0.91 all 10 seeds — NOT washed out; refuges supply local crowding) but is ORTHOGONAL to it: ON vs OFF indistinguishable on escape (both bimodal, means 1.498 vs 1.168 within sd~1.3), attack (~2.0), and coexistence (0/10 extinct) — contest does NOT buffer the Exp-259 prey-escape collapse. The washed-out hypothesis is falsified; the buffering hypothesis is unsupported.
- Verifier: controller designed + ran the experiment (multiprocessing), adjudicated PER-SEED itself (mean-of-opposites-guard): caught that the 2-seed smoke's apparent buffering was a mean-of-opposites artifact, confirmed both Part-B arms are bimodal with indistinguishable distributions, and confirmed aggr robustly emerges in all 10 ON seeds. OFF arm reproduces the expected bistable predator-dominance (Exp-259/261/262). exp264.txt is fully script-generated (R1 reproducibility lesson applied).
- Next: R3 — cannibalism / intraguild predation bridge (a forager that evolves to eat conspecifics BECOMES a predator -> emergent ROLES "what is a predator/prey"). Optional R2 follow-up: a refuge-free regime to test whether predation then COUPLES to intraspecific crowding (the refuges may be what decouples them here).
- trace: experiments/exp264_contest_in_context.py -> experiments/outputs/exp264.txt (multiprocessing basin map + 3-trait co-evolution contest ON/OFF; reuses ecology/patchmosaic.py contest substrate + exp263.invasion; applies skill mean-of-opposites-guard)

## Exp 265 — R2 follow-up: REFUGES decouple intraspecific aggression from predation — with refuges aggression emerges regardless of predation pressure; refuge-free it COUPLES (declines as a strong predator suppresses prey density) (POSITIVE-SINGLE / NEW INSIGHT). Direction: emergent-intraspecific-competition.
- Plain: R2 (Exp 264) found intraspecific aggression robustly co-emerged even under a co-evolving predator that suppressed GLOBAL prey density to N/K~0.05 — explained as the refuge patches crowding LOCALLY. This tests that explanation directly: REMOVE the refuge (keep migration+asynchrony for coexistence, the Exp-258 MIG_ONLY_ASYNC rescue) and ask whether aggression's emergence now COUPLES to predation pressure. 3-trait co-evolution (escape+aggr+attack), contest ON, refuge_mode {per_patch, none} x attack_a {0.01,0.03,0.05,0.07}, 10 seeds, horizon 3000.
- Consolidation vs new insight: POSITIVE-SINGLE / NEW INSIGHT. Confirms + mechanistically explains the R2 orthogonality: it is the REFUGES that decouple intraspecific crowding from global predation. NEW insight: a clean refuge x predation INTERACTION — spatial refuge structure determines whether intraspecific competition is independent of, or coupled to, interspecific predation.
- Question: does removing the refuge make aggression's emergence COUPLE to predation pressure? PREDICTION: refuge-free aggr@end DECLINES as attack_a rises (washed out once a strong predator suppresses prey below K); WITH refuge aggr@end stays HIGH across predation. FALSIFIER: if refuge-free aggr@end stays high across predation just like the refuge case, refuges are NOT the decoupler and the R2 orthogonality has another cause.
- Setup: experiments/exp265_refuge_free_coupling.py (multiprocessing). Same regime as R2 Part B (migration=0.05, async rotating amp=0.4, K_pred=40, hmax=0.05, escape_cost=0.15, contest_cost=0.10, contest_seize=0.50) with refuge_mode and attack_a as the two varied axes. FRESH seeds 730-739. Per-seed dispersion reported (mean-of-opposites-guard).
- Result: POSITIVE — the interaction holds. REFUGE (per_patch): aggr@end stays HIGH and ~flat across predation — 0.852, 0.804, 0.765, 0.830 at attack_a 0.01/0.03/0.05/0.07 (N/K 0.330 -> 0.124), 0/10 extinct everywhere (DECOUPLED: aggression emerges regardless of predation, refuge patches crowd locally). REFUGE-FREE (none): aggr@end DECLINES with predation — 0.734, 0.459, 0.396, 0.430 (N/K 0.219 -> 0.058), with 4/10 EXTINCT at attack_a=0.07 (COUPLED: as the predator suppresses global density, crowding falls and contest stops paying). At attack_a=0.05 the contrast is direct: refuge aggr=0.765 vs refuge-free aggr=0.396 (~2x). Refuge-free is variable/bimodal at the transition (a0.05 per-seed spans 0.06-0.83 — some seeds aggr emerges, some wash out: a stochastic crowding threshold).
- Implication: REFUGES decouple intraspecific competition from interspecific predation. Where prey have a spatial refuge (predator-access limited), they crowd LOCALLY regardless of how hard the global predator thins them, so intraspecific aggression emerges + persists independent of predation pressure (the R2 orthogonality). Remove the refuge and prey crowding is set by global density = controlled by predation, so aggression COUPLES: it emerges under weak predation (prey crowd) and washes out / the system collapses under strong predation. So spatial refuge structure is the knob that makes intraspecific and interspecific competition independent vs coupled — a concrete, mechanistic explanation of the R2 finding.
- Honest caveat: POSITIVE-SINGLE / NEW INSIGHT, scoped. (1) Refuge-free aggr@end at the transition (attack_a 0.03-0.05) is VARIABLE / partly bimodal per-seed (some seeds aggr emerges, some wash out) — read on per-seed (mean-of-opposites-guard), so the refuge-free value is a "partially/variably emerges", not a clean uniform low. (2) Refuge-free strong predation (attack_a=0.07) starts to COLLAPSE (4/10 extinct) — the decline there is partly viability loss, not only contest wash-out. (3) The DECOUPLING (refuge aggr flat ~0.8 across predation, 0/10 extinct) is clean and robust; the COUPLING (refuge-free decline) is clear in the means + N/K trend with the noted transition variability. (4) Trait expressibility by-construction; the result is the emergent refuge x predation interaction. Self-grade: POSITIVE-SINGLE.
- Verdict: POSITIVE-SINGLE — refuges decouple intraspecific aggression from predation. REFUGE: aggr@end flat ~0.77-0.85 across attack_a 0.01-0.07 (0/10 extinct) — decoupled. REFUGE-FREE: aggr@end declines 0.734 -> 0.459 -> 0.396 as predation rises + global N/K falls (0.219 -> 0.074), collapsing at attack_a=0.07 (4/10 extinct) — coupled. At attack_a=0.05 refuge sustains aggression (0.765) where refuge-free loses it (0.396). This is the mechanistic explanation of the R2 orthogonality: spatial refuge structure makes intraspecific competition independent of interspecific predation.
- Verifier: controller designed + ran the experiment (multiprocessing), adjudicated PER-SEED itself (mean-of-opposites-guard): confirmed the refuge cells are tightly high (decoupled) and the refuge-free cells decline with predation (coupled) with a variable/bimodal transition + viability loss at the strongest predation. exp265.txt is fully script-generated.
- Next: R3 — cannibalism / intraguild predation -> emergent ROLES (a forager that evolves to eat conspecifics BECOMES a predator). The refuge x competition coupling is now mechanistically understood.
- trace: experiments/exp265_refuge_free_coupling.py -> experiments/outputs/exp265.txt (refuge x predation grid, 3-trait co-evolution; reuses ecology/patchmosaic.py contest substrate; applies skill mean-of-opposites-guard)

## Exp 266 — R3: predator/prey ROLES do NOT emerge from cannibalism — a single predatoriness trait couples OFFENSE and INVULNERABILITY, so aggression runs away to the max (everyone an unkillable cannibal); no stable prey niche, no polymorphism (NEGATIVE / NEW INSIGHT). Direction: emergent-intraspecific-competition.
- Plain: R3 asks the human's "what IS a predator/prey" question — do distinct predator vs prey ROLES EMERGE from a single population? Added a gated cannibalism / intraguild-predation mechanic to ecology/patchmosaic.py (enable_cannibalism, byte-identical OFF, golden-guarded, T28): a predatory prey (high aggr) kills+consumes a conspecific (kill_prob = aggr_i*(1-aggr_j), frequency-dependent — predators eat NON-predators) for a reproduction boost, while being predatory trades off against foraging (birth *= 1 - cannibal_cost*aggr). Single population (n_pred0=0, step()-driven), aggr free-evolving from aggr0=0.3. Does aggr DIVERGE into a predator clade + a prey clade (roles), or converge?
- Consolidation vs new insight: NEGATIVE / NEW INSIGHT. The hypothesis (roles emerge via cannibalism) is FALSIFIED. NEW insight: with a SINGLE predatoriness trait, max predatoriness (aggr=1.0) is BOTH the best killer AND unkillable (others' kill_prob on it = aggr_j*(1-1.0)=0), so it confers offense AND invulnerability -> directional runaway to the ceiling, no stable prey niche -> no role differentiation. Emergent roles would require DECOUPLING offense from defense (separate predatoriness + vulnerability/defense axes).
- Question: does aggr DIVERGE into a stable bimodal predator/prey polymorphism (emergent roles)? FALSIFIER: if aggr stays unimodal (one clade / a single converged level), roles do not differentiate.
- Setup: experiments/exp266_emergent_roles.py (multiprocessing). Single prey population (n_pred0=0, driven by step() with prey-only extinction — run() halts on zero-predators), enable_cannibalism, enable_contest=False, aggr0=0.3, mutation_rate=0.15, cannibal_gain=0.5, cannibal_cost sweep {0.05,0.10,0.20}, horizon 3000, seeds 740-749. Measure the END aggr DISTRIBUTION per seed (clade fractions + percentiles; Sarle bimodality coefficient reported but UNRELIABLE at a ceiling pile).
- Result: NEGATIVE — aggr CONVERGES to the ceiling. mean aggr = 1.000 (std 0.000) at ALL cannibal_cost {0.05,0.10,0.20}, in ALL 10 seeds: 100.0% "predator" (aggr>0.5), 0.0% "prey" (aggr<0.2), p10/p50/p90 = 1.00/1.00/1.00, 0/10 extinct. A single spike at the aggr=1.0 boundary — NOT a bimodal predator/prey split. (The Sarle bimodality coefficient reads 0.63-0.99 but that is a CEILING-PILE ARTIFACT — a boundary spike + a small mutational tail inflates skew; the percentiles p10=p50=p90=1.00 + clade fractions 100/0 prove a single converged clade. The bimod coefficient is unreliable at a distribution boundary.)
- Implication: emergent predator/prey ROLES do NOT arise from this cannibalism mechanic — and the reason is mechanistically clear + general: when ONE trait (predatoriness) governs both how well you eat others AND how well you resist being eaten, the optimum is "max it" (be the unkillable apex), so the population runs away to a monomorphic max-predator instead of splitting into predator + prey clades. There is no stable "prey" niche because any prey can become invulnerable by raising aggr. Stable roles need offense and defense to be DECOUPLED (so that being a good predator does NOT also make you a good prey-survivor) — a frequency-dependent trade-off that this single-trait mechanic lacks. So "what is a predator/prey" does not self-organize here; the role wall is OFFENSE/DEFENSE COUPLING.
- Honest caveat: NEGATIVE / NEW INSIGHT, scoped. (1) The result is robust (10 seeds x 3 costs all -> aggr=1.0, 0 extinct) and the mechanism is explicit (offense/defense coupling via the kill_prob = aggr_i*(1-aggr_j) form). (2) A different vulnerability function (decoupled defense) might yield roles — this is a property of THIS minimal mechanic, not a claim that roles can never emerge. (3) Methodological catch: the Sarle bimodality coefficient is fooled by a boundary/ceiling pile (reads "bimodal" 0.6-0.99 when the distribution is a single spike at 1.0) — adjudicated on percentiles + clade fractions instead (refines mean-of-opposites-guard: distribution-shape stats can lie at a boundary). (4) cannibalism substrate is byte-identical OFF (goldens preserved, T28).
- Verdict: NEGATIVE / NEW INSIGHT — predator/prey roles do NOT emerge from single-trait cannibalism. aggr converges to the ceiling (1.000, std 0.000, 100% predator / 0% prey, 0/10 extinct) at every cannibal_cost {0.05,0.10,0.20}. The mechanistic reason: a single predatoriness trait couples offense and invulnerability (aggr=1.0 is unkillable), so selection runs away to a monomorphic max-predator with no stable prey niche. Emergent roles require decoupling offense from defense. (The bimod coefficient is a ceiling-pile artifact; adjudicated on percentiles/clade fractions.)
- Verifier: controller designed the cannibalism substrate (verified byte-identity goldens d063c91+790a8499 himself, T28 guards OFF/live), designed + ran the experiment (multiprocessing), and adjudicated the raw aggr DISTRIBUTION per seed — caught that the Sarle bimodality coefficient (0.63-0.99) was a ceiling-pile artifact and read the truth off the percentiles (p10=p50=p90=1.00) + clade fractions (100% pred / 0% prey), confirming convergence not roles, robust across 10 seeds x 3 costs.
- Next: R3b (optional) — decouple offense from defense (a separate vulnerability/defense trait, or a costed apex so high-aggr is genuinely vulnerable) so a stable prey niche can exist; then re-test for emergent roles. OR close the direction with the arc R1 (emerges) -> R2 (orthogonal) -> R2-followup (refuges decouple) -> R3 (roles need offense/defense decoupling).
- trace: experiments/exp266_emergent_roles.py -> experiments/outputs/exp266.txt (gated enable_cannibalism in ecology/patchmosaic.py, byte-identical OFF + T28; single-population step()-driven; adjudicated on distribution percentiles/clade-fractions not the boundary-fooled bimod coefficient)
